title: Make provider routing vendor-neutral for open source
summary: Remove maintainer-specific Grok preference from public defaults while preserving existing controllerHome/user configuration and GUI routing controls.
goals: Derive public default executor routing from capabilities, health and user-configured provider priority instead of preferring Grok by name.
Preserve existing provider and routing configuration files without resetting user choices.
Keep GUI provider/routing configuration authoritative and document config locations and precedence.
Replace provider-specific readiness summaries with neutral configured-provider summaries.
nonGoals: Change provider API implementations or credentials handling.
Expand the local Task Run agent list beyond its existing supported executors.
acceptanceCriteria: A fresh installation has no Grok-specific default repair provider or privileged routing position.
Existing controllerHome provider/routing JSON remains authoritative and is not migrated or overwritten.
GUI can still enable, disable, prioritize providers and edit per-intent routing orders.
Focused provider-routing tests, typecheck, controller-v8 and release-readiness pass.

tasks.title: Neutralize provider defaults and routing
tasks.objective: Implement vendor-neutral default provider priority/routing, neutral readiness copy, documentation and focused regression coverage while preserving existing user configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Fresh defaults do not privilege Grok by provider ID.
Configured routing order and provider priority continue to override defaults.
No existing controllerHome config file is rewritten by merely reading defaults.
Open-source configuration docs explain GUI and JSON config paths.
notes: Verification evidence is incomplete or failed: One or more acceptance criteria are missing or failed.
Verification evidence passed and medium_risk_change policy auto-completed the Task.

commandEvidence.stdout: Provider configuration, capability routing, automatic routing reset, and Dashboard semantic tests passed.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Fresh defaults do not privilege Grok by provider ID.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Local CLI providers share equal default priority, remote API providers share equal default priority, and generated routing contains no named Grok default.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Configured routing order and provider priority continue to override defaults.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Regression tests cover explicit route precedence, provider-priority generated routes, and GUI/API automatic-routing reset.

acceptanceResults.criterion: No existing controllerHome config file is rewritten by merely reading defaults.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Read-path test confirms executor-routing.json remains absent after generated routing is read.
Grok-specific readiness sentences were replaced by generic configured remote-provider status and a negative regression assertion.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Open-source configuration docs explain GUI and JSON config paths.
acceptanceResults.evidence: docs/operations/provider-configuration.md documents controllerHome paths, precedence, GUI controls, secrets and automatic routing.
